case_title: BRENDA BESDANSKY VS. STEVE & IRMA ASTI, IT., LLC ET AL

docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF MOTION OF DEFENDANTS CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425 16 ANTI SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE THE COMPLAINT OF BRENDA BESDANSKY (TRANSACTION ID # 65656538) FILED BY DEFENDANT STEVE & IRMA ASTI, IT., LLC SCARABOSIO, RAYMOND JOHN SCARABOSIO, WILLIAM SCARABOSIO, NICHOLAS JACKSON GROUP PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC. DECLARATION OF BRENDA BESDANSKY IN SUPPORT OF HER OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF COMPLAINT PER CCP 425.16 (TRANSACTION ID # 65783171) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BESDANSKY, BRENDA

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT STEVE & IRMA ASTI, IT., LLC, RAYMOND SCARABOSIO, WILLIAM SCARABOSIO, NICHOLAS SCARABOSIO, JACKSON GROUP PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC. ANTI SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE THE COMPLAINT OF BRENDA BESDANSKY IS GRANTED AS TO THE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT SUBJECT TO THIS MOTION (MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION 3:2-4:4). THE CLAIMS ARISING OUT OF SERVICE OF AN "EVICTION NOTICE" (E.G. COMPLAINT, PARAGRAPH 33) INCORPORATED INTO EVERY CAUSE OF ACTION AND EXPRESSLY ADDITIONALLY STATED AS THE BASIS FOR RELIEF IN CAUSES OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION, VIOLATION OF SFRO 37.9, VIOLATION OF CIVIL CODE 1940.2, FRAUD, AND UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES FALL WITHIN THE FIRST PRONG OF THE ANTI-SLAPP. PLAINTIFF WAS PROVIDED AN OPPORTUNITY TO SUBMIT A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF TO SHOW PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS BY IDENTIFYING EVIDENCE THAT SUPPORTS EACH ELEMENT OF THE CLAIM ARISING OUT OF THE SERVICE OF EVICTION NOTICE ALONE (AS OPPOSED TO OTHER WRONGFUL CONDUCT ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT). PLAINTIFF'S SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF, AFTER LISTING THE REQUIRED ELEMENTS FOR EACH CAUSE OF ACTION, REFERENCES EVIDENCE TO ONLY SOME, BUT NOT ALL, OF THE ELEMENTS. THEREFORE, PLAINTIFF FAILED TO CARRY HER BURDEN TO SHOW PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS ON THE CLAIMS ARISING SOLELY OUT OF THE SERVICE OF THE "EVICTION NOTICE." THIS RULING HAS NO IMPACT ON OTHER CLAIMS WITHIN THE SAME CAUSES OF ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, NOT REPORTED (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT STEVE & IRMA ASTI, IT., LLC, RAYMOND SCARABOSIO, WILLIAM SCARABOSIO, NICHOLAS SCARABOSIO, JACKSON GROUP PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501,NOTICE OF MOTION OF DEFENDANTS CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425 16 ANTI SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE THE COMPLAINT OF BRENDA BESDANSKY IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 14, 2020. THE CLAIMS ARISING OUT OF SERVICE OF AN "EVICTION NOTICE" (E.G. COMPLAINT, PARAGRAPH 33) INCORPORATED INTO EVERY CAUSES OF ACTION AND EXPRESSLY ADDITIONALLY STATED AS THE BASIS FOR RELIEF IN CAUSES OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION, VIOLATION OF SFRO 37.9, VIOLATION OF CIVIL CODE 1940.2, FRAUD, AND UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES FALL WITHIN THE FIRST PRONG OF THE ANTI-SLAPP. THE BURDEN IS ON THE PLAINTIFF TO SHOW THE PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS ON THE CLAIMS ARISING OUT OF THE SERVICE OF THE NOTICE, I.E. PRESENT EVIDENCE AS TO ALL ELEMENTS OF THE CLAIM (FOR EXAMPLE EVIDENCE AS TO EACH ELEMENT OF FRAUD CLAIM ARISING OUT OF ALLEGATIONS CONTAINED IN PARAGRAPHS 33, 107, 152) "BASED ON DEFENDANTS' WRONGFUL CONDUCT" OTHER THAN SERVICE OF THE NOTICE AS IT RELATES TO THE PARAGRAPHS SUBJECT TO THIS MOTION. BY NO LATER THAN AUGUST 7, 2020 PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF IN A TABLE FORMAT MATCHING THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION WITH THE ELEMENTS OF EACH CLAIMS ARISING OUT OF THE SERVICE OF THE NOTICE. FORMAT OF THE TABLE: COLUMN ONE - ELEMENT OF A CAUSE OF ACTION; COLUM TWO - CITATION TO EVIDENCE, E.G. DECLARATION OF JANE DOE, PARAGRAPH 3, LINES 2-5. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-16-2020 Set For Trial On Jun-07-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion Of Defendants Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425 16 Anti Slapp Motion To Strike The Complaint Of Brenda Besdansky
Off Calendar Aug-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STEVE & IRMA ASTI, IT., LLC, RAYMOND SCARABOSIO, WILLIAM SCARABOSIO, NICHOLAS SCARABOSIO, JACKSON GROUP PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC. Anti Slapp Motion To Strike The Complaint Of Brenda Besdansky is GRANTED as to the portions of the Complaint subject to this motion (Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Support of Motion 3:2-4:4). The claims arising out of service of an "eviction notice" (e.g. Complaint, paragraph 33) incorporated into every cause of action and expressly additionally stated as the basis for relief in causes of action for retaliation, violation of SFRO 37.9, violation of civil code 1940.2, fraud, and unfair business practices fall within the first prong of the ANTI-SLAPP. Plaintiff was provided an opportunity to submit a supplemental brief to show probability of prevailing on the merits by identifying evidence that supports each element of the claim arising out of the service of eviction notice alone (as opposed to other wrongful conduct alleged in the Complaint). Plaintiff's supplemental brief, after listing the required elements for each cause of action, references evidence to only some, but not all, of the elements. Therefore, Plaintiff failed to carry her burden to show probability of prevailing on the merits on the claims arising solely out of the service of the "eviction notice." This ruling has no impact on other claims within the same causes of action. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: W. Trupek, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion Of Defendants Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425 16 Anti Slapp Motion To Strike The Complaint Of Brenda Besdansky
Off Calendar Jul-31-2020 Continued To Aug-14-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Marina Franco, 415.567.9600 for Defendants Steve and Irma Asit, IT, LLC. (Court call)
No other appearances.

Discussed. Defendant seeks clarification as to wether or not they need to provide additional flings.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Notice Of Motion Of Defendants Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425 16 Anti Slapp Motion To Strike The Complaint Of Brenda Besdansky is continued to August 14, 2020. The claims arising out of service of an "eviction notice" (e.g. Complaint, paragraph 33) incorporated into every causes of action and expressly additionally stated as the basis for relief in causes of action for retaliation, violation of SFRO 37.9, violation of civil code 1940.2, fraud, and unfair business practices fall within the first prong of the ANTI-SLAPP. The burden is on the Plaintiff to show the probability of prevailing on the merits on the claims arising out of the service of the notice, i.e. present evidence as to all elements of the claim (for example evidence as to each element of fraud claim arising out of allegations contained in paragraphs 33, 107, 152) "based on defendants' wrongful conduct" other than service of the notice as it relates to the paragraphs subject to this motion. By no later than August 7, 2020 Plaintiff to submit a supplemental brief in a table format matching the evidence submitted in support of opposition with the elements of each claims arising out of the service of the notice. Format of the table: Column One - Element of a cause of action; Colum Two - citation to evidence, e.g. Declaration of Jane Doe, paragraph 3, lines 2-5. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
